main_turf.bias_notes: Right-handed, oval-shaped track with a steady, punishing climb over the final 3 furlongs. It is a severe test of stamina, especially when the ground is soft.
main_turf.post_bias: In sprints (5F-6F), high draw numbers (stands-side) historically hold an advantage on soft or heavy ground.

handicapping_angles.the_stamina_test: Downgrade one-dimensional speed horses (E types). Because of the uphill finish, horses dropping down in distance (e.g., from 7F down to 6F) often out-finish horses stretching out.
handicapping_angles.stands_side_rail: When the track is rated Soft or Heavy, look for horses drawn high that possess the tactical speed to grab the stands-side rail.
handicapping_angles.class_drop_shippers: Upgrade horses shipping from Tier 2 tracks like Newmarket or Ascot dropping into Class 4 or 5 handicaps here.
